Before touching parse-count instrumentation or user-facing error messages, confirm whether a run record was actually created and persisted. If no run exists, parsing instrumentation is irrelevant — the break is in run creation, submi  ·  Do first: Query the system for run records tied to this live meet. If zero records exist, the bug is in run creation or persistence — investigate there. ## 🔁 Regressed (a closed defect came back — fix first)

### [HIGH] Cards lack round/heat label — prelim and final swims appear as impossible duplicates · `4fe01d0e3bb7`

- **Category:** `council:blind_spot`
- **Where:** `output`
- **Seen:** 2× · first `2026-06-01T21:43:18+00:00` · last `2026-06-10T20:40:41+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Expected:** Each card identifies the round (e.g. Prelim, Final, Heat 3) so that two legitimate first-place finishes by the same swimmer in different rounds are distinguishable
- **Actual:** Cards show swimmer, event, medal, and time but no round or heat designation; a prelim gold and a final gold by the same swimmer are rendered as duplicate impossible medals with no context
- **Evidence:**

```
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
Chairman analysis: '27.42 / 27.24 split is almost certainly prelims and finals, not a deduplication error — both are genuine first-place finishes in their respective rounds'; source file MISM-2024-RESULTS.PDF must be read before any fix is written
```

### [HIGH] Per-swimmer data lookup failure is the likely shared root cause of issues [0] and [1] · `a7bf971f553b`

- **Category:** `council:blind_spot`
- **Where:** `semantic:output/high`
- **Seen:** 3× · first `2026-06-01T11:48:25+00:00` · last `2026-06-11T20:32:34+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Expected:** Issues [0] (wrong caption body) and [1] (confidence=medium on unambiguous gold medals) investigated as a single defect in the per-swimmer data pipeline rather than two independent bugs
- **Actual:** Filed as two separate issues; fixing the per-swimmer data lookup for [0] will almost certainly resolve the confidence downgrade in [1] as a side effect, meaning the true issue count is smaller than it appears
- **Evidence:**

```
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
Chairman verdict: 'If the per-swimmer data lookup is failing and falling back to meet-aggregate stats, that same failure explains why individual achievements can't be verified with high confidence, producing medium across the board. These aren't two independent bugs.'
```

### [HIGH] Primary CTA says 'Sign in' not 'Sign up', misdirecting first-time visitors · `0da08f047440`

- **Category:** `semantic:user_brain`
- **Where:** `home / sign-up`
- **Seen:** 2× · first `2026-06-17T11:09:03+00:00` · last `2026-06-18T10:47:46+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Repro:**
  1. Reproduce the flow (result: live:inspected-0-runs-no-content) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above
- **Expected:** A first-time volunteer sees a clear 'Sign up' or 'Create account' button as the dominant action
- **Actual:** The first interactive link reads 'Sign in →', implying an existing account is required; the new-user path appears below it as a visually subordinate line with no label indicating it is for new users
- **Evidence:**

```
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
Sign in →
Set up my organisation
```

### [HIGH] a11y: Form elements must have labels (label) · `6aaf48f23c73`

- **Category:** `a11y`
- **Where:** `/operator/commercial`
- **Seen:** 22× · first `2026-06-13T04:07:27+00:00` · last `2026-06-18T20:36:32+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Suspected source:** `axe:label`
- **Repro:**
  1. Open /operator/commercial
  2. Run axe-core; rule 'label' flags 1 node(s)
- **Expected:** The page meets the WCAG rule axe-core checks
- **Actual:** axe-core 'label' violation (critical) on 1 element(s)
- **Evidence:**

```
axe-core rule: label
help: Form elements must have labels
helpUrl: https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.12/label?application=axeAPI
example targets:
  input[type="text"]
```

- **Prior fix:** `https://github.com/elijahkendrick04/MediaHub/pull/573` (regressed 2026-06-16T21:01:56+00:00)

### [HIGH] Brand/club-site setup required before content generates — not disclosed near the sign-up CTA · `b377013ac602`

- **Category:** `semantic:user_brain`
- **Where:** `onboarding`
- **Seen:** 4× · first `2026-06-18T10:47:46+00:00` · last `2026-06-23T20:17:36+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Repro:**
  1. Reproduce the flow (result: live:inspected-0-runs-no-content) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above
- **Expected:** Upfront disclosure that the first session requires connecting a club site, social profiles, and a brand kit before any content can be produced, with a rough time estimate
- **Actual:** The hero copy promises immediate output ('Results in. Stories, reels, graphics, captions out.') but How It Works reveals the engine must first read club site, social profiles and brand kit. A volunteer who signs up expecting to upload a file and instantly get a post will hit an uncommunicated setup wall.
- **Evidence:**

```
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
MediaHub reads the sources you already have: your club site, social profiles and brand kit (your logo, colours and fonts)
```

### [HIGH] Accepted file formats never stated anywhere on the page · `4acb3bac94a2`

- **Category:** `semantic:user_brain`
- **Where:** `upload / onboarding`
- **Seen:** 2× · first `2026-06-22T12:17:05+00:00` · last `2026-06-23T20:17:36+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Repro:**
  1. Reproduce the flow (result: live:inspected-0-runs-no-content) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above
- **Expected:** A clear list of supported file formats near the upload CTA or in How It Works, so a volunteer knows before signing up whether their results export will work
- **Actual:** The only format visible anywhere on the page is a filename in a demo graphic. No format list, no tooltip, no footnote. A volunteer with a Swimtopia CSV, a PDF printout, or a Meet Mobile export has no idea whether the product accepts their file.
- **Evidence:**

```
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
spring-open.hy3
```

### [HIGH] Create-to-run pipeline has never successfully executed or persisted a run record in production · `b6f86c8a8de9`

- **Category:** `council:blind_spot`
- **Where:** `functional/pipeline`
- **Seen:** 6× · first `2026-06-02T14:40:53+00:00` · last `2026-06-24T03:58:24+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Expected:** Triggering a create flow against a real meet file should produce and persist a run record in the live system.
- **Actual:** Zero run records exist in the live system; the pipeline may never have fired rather than having fired and returned empty results.
- **Evidence:**

```
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
"The flow token is `inspected-0-runs-no-content`. That means zero runs exist in the live system — not that a run was executed and produced zero cards. These are categorically different failure modes: A run that produced zero cards → possible pipeline or data-matching issue; Zero runs in the system → the create-to-run pipeline has never successfully executed or persisted in production."
```

### [HIGH] Live inspection query silently excludes runs that exist in the live system — scoping or permissions filter bug · `f9647b08c0e5`

- **Category:** `council:blind_spot`
- **Where:** `functional`
- **Seen:** 4× · first `2026-06-10T20:40:41+00:00` · last `2026-06-24T14:44:25+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Expected:** A live inspection of an organisation should return the runs belonging to that organisation that are already recorded in the system
- **Actual:** Live inspection returns zero runs despite the live counter showing 053 TOTAL RUNS — the pipeline has executed successfully before, so the zero-result is a filter exclusion, not an engine failure
- **Evidence:**

```
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
The [8] counter says: '053 TOTAL RUNS / 5243 MOMENTS DETECTED', live system. All three advisors treated [8] purely as a marketing vanity metric and moved on. That was wrong. Those 53 runs are diagnostic evidence. If 53 runs already exist in the live system, then [0] is not a data-absence bug — the pipeline has run before, successfully, for someone. The live inspection returning zero is therefore a scoping failure, a permissions boundary, or a silent query filter — not a broken engine.
```

### [HIGH] Review queue shows 3 ranked moments but only displays 1 card with no navigation · `f51fbb0c9424`

- **Category:** `semantic:user_brain`
- **Where:** `review queue`
- **Seen:** 7× · first `2026-05-31T19:29:01+00:00` · last `2026-06-24T19:58:40+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Repro:**
  1. Reproduce the flow (result: live:inspected-0-runs-no-content) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above
- **Expected:** Some indication of how to move between moments — pagination, a queue count, next/previous controls — so the volunteer knows there are more cards to approve
- **Actual:** The demo reports '42 SWIMS READ · 3 MOMENTS RANKED' but the REVIEW step shows only the Tom Davies card with EDIT / REJECT / APPROVE buttons and nothing pointing to the other two moments. A volunteer approving this card would not know whether the queue is done or what comes next.
- **Evidence:**

```
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
42 SWIMS READ · 3 MOMENTS RANKED
```

- **Prior fix:** `https://github.com/elijahkendrick04/MediaHub/pull/1027` (regressed 2026-06-24T19:58:40+00:00)

### [HIGH] No SIGN UP entry point in the main navigation · `76a8133ead76`

- **Category:** `semantic:user_brain`
- **Where:** `navigation / sign-up`
- **Seen:** 4× · first `2026-06-12T20:20:34+00:00` · last `2026-06-24T19:58:40+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Repro:**
  1. Reproduce the flow (result: live:inspected-0-runs-no-content) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above
- **Expected:** A SIGN UP button or link in the top navigation so a first-time visitor can find it without scrolling the full page
- **Actual:** The nav bar lists 'HOME / CREATE / MEDIA LIBRARY / ELEMENTS / MY SEASON / SETTINGS / PRICING / LOG IN' — only LOG IN is present. Sign-up is only available by scrolling to the hero and finding 'Sign up →'
- **Evidence:**

```
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
HOME
CREATE
MEDIA LIBRARY
ELEMENTS
MY SEASON
SETTINGS
PRICING
LOG IN
```

- **Prior fix:** `https://github.com/elijahkendrick04/MediaHub/pull/646` (regressed 2026-06-24T19:58:40+00:00)

### [HIGH] Pipeline trigger never fired — zero runs recorded, not a zero-card output · `3fc17fa1bede`

- **Category:** `council:blind_spot`
- **Where:** `semantic:functional`
- **Seen:** 3× · first `2026-06-17T15:48:18+00:00` · last `2026-06-25T09:34:15+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Expected:** At least one pipeline execution should be recorded per meet ingestion cycle so that a zero-card result can be distinguished from a never-triggered run.
- **Actual:** Tester metadata indicates zero pipeline runs were ever dispatched for the live organisations, pointing to a failure at the trigger, scheduler, or ingestion layer rather than in output processing.
- **Evidence:**

```
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
"The tester-internal token reads inspected-0-runs-no-content — zero runs, not one run that returned zero cards. These are different failure modes... A system where no pipeline run was ever triggered is an upstream infrastructure failure — broken job scheduler, missing meet ingestion, no trigger fired at all. The council answered the wrong version of the [0] question."
```

### [HIGH] Accepted file formats never stated — volunteer with non-.hy3 results has no guidance · `09e7092403de`

- **Category:** `semantic:user_brain`
- **Where:** `home / how it works`
- **Seen:** 2× · first `2026-06-18T04:20:10+00:00` · last `2026-06-25T14:45:56+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Repro:**
  1. Reproduce the flow (result: live:inspected-0-runs-no-content) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above
- **Expected:** The page should state what formats are accepted for upload (e.g. HY-TEK .hy3, Excel, PDF) so I know my file will work before I sign up
- **Actual:** The demo silently shows a .hy3 file but no list of accepted formats appears anywhere on the page; a volunteer with a spreadsheet or PDF results sheet would have no idea if this product even supports their file
- **Evidence:**

```
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
spring-open.hy3
```

### [MEDIUM] Review stage shows only the Story card format — FEED and REEL formats never reviewed · `deeefa4ee496`

- **Category:** `semantic:user_brain`
- **Where:** `review / approve flow`
- **Seen:** 9× · first `2026-06-12T10:25:27+00:00` · last `2026-06-23T03:55:05+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Repro:**
  1. Reproduce the flow (result: live:inspected-0-runs-no-content) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above
- **Expected:** Volunteer can review and approve each of the four promised output formats (Story, Feed, Reel, Caption) before download, or understands that approving the moment approves all formats.
- **Actual:** The review step shows 'STORY CARD · CAPTION DRAFT' only. The approve step then lists 'STORY / FEED / REEL / CAPTION' as ready to download, but the volunteer never reviewed or approved the Feed or Reel — they just appear. No explanation of this skip.
- **Evidence:**

```
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
STORY CARD · CAPTION DRAFT

EDIT
REJECT
APPROVE
```

- **Prior fix:** `https://github.com/elijahkendrick04/MediaHub/pull/845` (regressed 2026-06-23T03:55:05+00:00)

### [MEDIUM] Bulk review path for multiple moments not shown — volunteer may not know how many approvals are required · `bb2cbd8a2f96`

- **Category:** `semantic:user_brain`
- **Where:** `review / approval flow`
- **Seen:** 2× · first `2026-06-13T19:47:40+00:00` · last `2026-06-23T20:17:36+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Repro:**
  1. Reproduce the flow (result: live:inspected-0-runs-no-content) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above
- **Expected:** Clear indication of how many items need individual review after a file is processed, and whether a bulk-approve option exists
- **Actual:** The detect step shows three ranked moments, but the review step depicts only one card with individual APPROVE action and no queue indicator, pagination, or 'approve all' option. A volunteer expecting to quickly approve 12 moments from a full meet would not know the effort required.
- **Evidence:**

```
AI judge (user_brain, confidence medium):
42 SWIMS READ · 3 MOMENTS RANKED
```

### [MEDIUM] Setup prerequisites hidden behind sign-up — volunteer expects to just upload a file · `17fc71f98d32`

- **Category:** `semantic:user_brain`
- **Where:** `onboarding / sign-up`
- **Seen:** 3× · first `2026-06-15T12:28:49+00:00` · last `2026-06-25T03:58:49+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Repro:**
  1. Reproduce the flow (result: live:inspected-0-runs-no-content) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above
- **Expected:** A first-time sign-up should immediately let me upload a results file and get content, matching the 'Results in. Content out.' promise.
- **Actual:** The engine actually requires reading 'your club site, social profiles and brand kit (your logo, colours and fonts)' before it can generate anything — a multi-step club-profile setup that is never disclosed on the sign-up CTA or in the onboarding flow shown.
- **Evidence:**

```
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
MediaHub reads your club website, social profiles, and brand guidelines, then writes captions, builds graphics, and renders motion videos in your voice. Set up once. Reuse forever.
```

## 🔴 Open bugs

### [HIGH] fetch 502 on /add-input · `6a9413187b26`

- **Category:** `network_error`
- **Where:** `/add-input`
- **Seen:** 1× · first `2026-06-25T14:45:56+00:00` · last `2026-06-25T14:45:56+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Repro:**
  1. Open https://mediahub-gzwc.onrender.com/add-input
- **Expected:** All same-origin sub-requests succeed
- **Actual:** fetch → 502: https://mediahub-gzwc.onrender.com/healthz
- **Evidence:**

### [HIGH] a11y: Select element must have an accessible name (select-name) · `3d7b7df57b3f`

- **Category:** `a11y`
- **Where:** `/organisation`
- **Seen:** 75× · first `2026-06-02T14:40:53+00:00` · last `2026-06-25T20:11:09+00:00`
- **Suspected source:** `axe:select-name`
- **Repro:**
  1. Open /organisation
  2. Run axe-core; rule 'select-name' flags 2 node(s)
- **Expected:** The page meets the WCAG rule axe-core checks
- **Actual:** axe-core 'select-name' violation (critical) on 2 element(s)
- **Evidence:**

```
axe-core rule: select-name
help: Select element must have an accessible name
helpUrl: https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.12/select-name?application=axeAPI
example targets:
  select[name="org_type"]
  select[name="language"]
```

## ⏳ Pending confirmation (subjective — not yet a bug)

_AI/judge findings seen too few times to open. The fixer IGNORES these; each sweep they recur bumps the count, and at `AUTOTEST_CONFIRM_SWEEPS` they become open bugs. One-shot noise decays out instead._

- [HIGH] All /healthz/* monitoring probes return 502 — health-check infrastructure is entirely unreachable · `621ec2aba449` (semantic:functional) — confirmed 0× since `2026-06-25T14:45:56+00:00` · not in latest run
- [HIGH] /export returns 502 — export feature is entirely inaccessible · `ecd675173bec` (semantic:functional) — confirmed 0× since `2026-06-25T14:45:56+00:00` · not in latest run
- [HIGH] /athletes and /athletes/consent.csv return 502 — athlete roster and GDPR consent download unavailable · `13ed2c05bb99` (semantic:functional) — confirmed 0× since `2026-06-25T14:45:56+00:00` · not in latest run
- [HIGH] /billing and /billing/confirm return 502 — payment flows broken · `f218c84e3a8f` (semantic:functional) — confirmed 0× since `2026-06-25T14:45:56+00:00` · not in latest run
- [HIGH] Run record may never be created or persisted — 'inspected-0-runs-no-content' token indicates upstream failure before parsing · `03ce2b0e61a1` (council:blind_spot) — confirmed 0× since `2026-06-25T20:11:09+00:00`
- [MEDIUM] Routes flip from 502 to 200 between crawl passes — backend intermittently unavailable · `c69c89d262cc` (semantic:functional) — confirmed 0× since `2026-06-25T14:45:56+00:00` · not in latest run

## 🟡 In progress (fix loop has an open PR — don't double-assign)

- [HIGH] No 'Sign Up' entry point in primary navigation · `bca7468e75a2` → PR https://github.com/elijahkendrick04/MediaHub/pull/1030

<details><summary>🗂️ Verified-fixed — retired with evidence (14)</summary>

- a11y: Documents must have <title> element to aid in navigation (document-title) · `33893e777a30` — verified via `48e913f` (tests/test_autotest_a11y.py::test_a11y_audit_gated_to_html_content_types); False positive. axe-core's document-title rule fired on the synthetic viewer Chromium renders for the non-HTML route /healthz/search; this is not a real WCAG document — the route serves JS/JSON, not an HTML page. Eliminated at source by the finder-precision fix (PR #443): the a11y audit is now gated to HTML responses. Loop-authored fix PR #433 closed as a false-positive fix. — by elijahkendrick04 (operator approval) via Claude Code — finder-precision fix PR #443 at 2026-06-13T19:39:44+00:00
- a11y: Documents must have <title> element to aid in navigation (document-title) · `ac6f77e15778` — verified via `48e913f` (tests/test_autotest_a11y.py::test_a11y_audit_gated_to_html_content_types); False positive. axe-core's document-title rule fired on the synthetic viewer Chromium renders for the non-HTML route /manifest.webmanifest; this is not a real WCAG document — the route serves JS/JSON, not an HTML page. - a11y: Documents must have <title> element to aid in navigation (document-title) · `cc9f715751f8` — verified via `48e913f` (tests/test_autotest_a11y.py::test_a11y_audit_gated_to_html_content_types); False positive. axe-core's document-title rule fired on the synthetic viewer Chromium renders for the non-HTML route /api/status; this is not a real WCAG document — the route serves JS/JSON, not an HTML page. - Upload and processing completed successfully but yielded no content cards. · `e883e6f0bbc7` — verified via `5c86706 (#196, product honest empty state) + this PR (finder precision: functional rubric v2)` (tests/test_autotest_empty_state_precision.py (8 passed); tests/test_review_empty_state_club_match.py (3 passed)); FALSE-POSITIVE: '/review 200 + 0 cards' is the CORRECT, explained club-mismatch empty state, not a contradiction. The functional judge mis-flagged it because content_summary omitted the swim-match counts; the fix gives the judge those counts so it no longer false-positives. — by LLM Council (finder-precision verdict, autotest/reports/council/) + human merge of this harness PR at 2026-05-31T16:50:03+00:00
- Implement clear, immediate, and actionable user feedback when no content cards are generated due to insufficient data. · `71e02f586c45` — verified via `5c86706 (#196)` (tests/test_review_empty_state_club_match.py (3 passed)); The real UX gap this finding requested — clear, actionable feedback when no cards are generated — is exactly what #196 shipped ('No swims matched your club', shows parsed count, links to re-run). — by LLM Council (finder-precision verdict, autotest/reports/council/) + human merge of this harness PR at 2026-05-31T16:50:03+00:00
- 4/6 recent runs produced ZERO content cards · `37d87cf5f107` — verified via `5c86706 (#196, product honest empty state) + this PR (finder precision: functional rubric v2)` (tests/test_autotest_empty_state_precision.py (8 passed); tests/test_review_empty_state_club_match.py (3 passed)); FALSE-POSITIVE: '/review 200 + 0 cards' is the CORRECT, explained club-mismatch empty state, not a contradiction. The functional judge mis-flagged it because content_summary omitted the swim-match counts; the fix gives the judge those counts so it no longer false-positives. — by LLM Council (finder-precision verdict, autotest/reports/council/) + human merge of this harness PR at 2026-05-31T16:50:03+00:00
